key_insight: A data-efficient continuous conditional diffusion model can generate realistic microstructures from continuous processing parameters, even with limited training data.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster surrogate-based process-structure exploration and microstructure design for materials optimization without expensive physics simulations.
method: Trains a denoising diffusion model on process-microstructure pairs.
Uses vicinal-loss to better handle sparse continuous process conditions.
Adds classifier-free guidance and DDIM sampling for efficient conditional generation.
evidence_signal: The model generated representative low-carbon steel microstructures conditioned on manganese composition, matching phase and grain morphology, grain size distribution, phase fraction, and interfacial area distribution.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how well the compact dataset covers the continuous process space, especially in data-sparse regions.
paper_title: Data-efficient continuous conditional denoising diffusion model for microstructure generation
